game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Playdead's LIMBO:** Players control a boy in a 2D side-scrolling world, navigating through a series of environmental puzzles and platforming challenges. Movement is controlled by swiping left or right on the left side of the screen, while swiping up on the left side initiates a jump. To interact with objects like crates, levers, or ropes, players tap and hold on the right side of the screen, allowing for pushing, pulling, or climbing actions. The game features a linear progression through distinct, interconnected areas, with difficulty scaling through increasingly complex physics-based puzzles and deadlier environmental hazards. Upon death, which is frequent and often a learning mechanic, the player instantly respawns at the last checkpoint, encouraging trial-and-error to advance through the narrative.
game_info.description: Navigate through a dark, atmospheric world as a young boy searching for his lost sister. Solve environmental puzzles, avoid deadly traps, and overcome platforming challenges. Push crates to reach higher ground, activate levers to open paths, and use your wits to survive the hazardous environment. Death is part of learning—each failure teaches you how to progress. Reach the final checkpoint to complete your journey.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left/Right: Move left or right
Arrow Up/Space: Jump
Z Key: Interact (push/pull crates, activate levers, grab ropes)
Enter: Start game
Esc: Pause/Unpause
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement mechanics and interaction with objects. Validates that the player can move, jump, and interact with crates and levers correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move right and left, jump to test physics, interact with the first crate to push it, and activate a lever. Monitor position changes and interaction states.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully moves, jumps with correct physics, pushes crate when holding Z, and activates lever. Position and game state updates are validated throughout.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the optimal strategy to complete the game from start to finish. This validates that the win condition is reachable and all puzzle mechanics work correctly.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Follow the optimal path through all checkpoints: push crates to create platforms, avoid spike traps, activate levers to open gates, use moving platforms, and reach the final checkpoint. Execute precise jumps and interactions.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player reaches the final checkpoint triggering GAME_OVER_WIN state. All puzzles are solved efficiently, no deaths occur with optimal play, and the game completion is validated.
